title: List of Acts and Regulations - Canada.ca
content:
## List of Acts and Regulations
- Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Act This Act authorizes the Minister of Health to issue administrative monetary penalties to enforce compliance with provisions of the Pest Control Products Act and associated regulations. Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Regulations Respecting the Pest Control Products Act and Regulations (SOR/2001-132; last amended 2022-01-01)
- Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Regulations Respecting the Pest Control Products Act and Regulations (SOR/2001-132; last amended 2022-01-01)
- Assisted Human Reproduction Act This Act addresses numerous ethical and health concerns arising from the use of assisted reproductive technologies. The Act prohibits a number of activities pertaining to assisted reproduction and related research, including cloning, in vitro embryo sex selection and payment for surrogacy. Some of the prohibited activities can be carried out if done in accordance with regulatory requirements that pertain to consent, testing donated sperm and ova for health and safety risks, and reimbursement of expenditures related to gamete donation and surrogacy. Administration and Enforcement (Assisted Human Reproduction Act) Regulations (SOR/2019-194) Consent for Use of Human Reproductive Material and In Vitro Embryos Regulations (SOR/2007-137; last amended 2019-12-26) Reimbursement Related to Assisted Human Reproduction Regulations (SOR/2019-193; last amended 2020-06-09) Safety of Sperm and Ova Regulations (SOR/2019-192; last amended 2022-09-27)

- Canada Health Act This Act establishes the criteria and conditions that provincial health insurance plans or extended health care services must meet to receive the full cash contributions under the Canada Health Transfer. Extra-billing and User Charges Information Regulations (SOR/86-259)
- Extra-billing and User Charges Information Regulations (SOR/86-259)
- Canada Consumer Product Safety Act The purpose of this Act is to protect the public by addressing or preventing dangers to human health or safety that are posed by consumer products in Canada, including those that circulate within Canada and those that are imported. - Cannabis Act This Act creates a strict legal framework for controlling the production, distribution, sale, importation, exportation, and possession of cannabis across Canada. The objectives of the Act are to prevent young persons from accessing cannabis, to protect public health and public safety by establishing strict product safety and product quality requirements, and to deter criminal activity by imposing serious criminal penalties for those operating outside the legal framework. Order Designating the Minister of Health as the Minister for the purpose of that Act (SI/2019-125) Cannabis Act (Police Enforcement) Regulations (SOR/2018-151; last amended 2022-07-15) Cannabis Fees Order (SOR/2018-198; last amended 2024-08-28) Cannabis for Medical Purposes Remission Order (SOR/2020-9; last amended 2020-01-22) Cannabis Regulations (SOR/2018-144; last amended 2025-03-12) Cannabis Tracking System Order (SOR/2019-202; last amended 2025-04-01) Industrial Hemp Regulations (SOR/2018-145; last amended 2025-03-12) Qualifications for Designation as Analyst Regulations (Cannabis) (SOR/2018-146; last amended 2018-10-17)

- Controlled Drugs and Substances Act This Act provides for the control of substances that can alter mental processes and that may produce harm to health and to society when diverted or used inappropriately. Except as authorized by regulation or via an exemption issued under Section 56 of the Act, activities, such as possession, sale, production, importation and exportation, involving substances listed in the Schedules to the Act (known as "controlled substances" and precursors) are prohibited. - Department of Health Act This Act sets out the powers, duties and functions of the Minister, which extend to all matters covering the promotion or preservation of the health of Canadians over which Parliament has jurisdiction. Potable Water on Board Trains, Vessels, Aircraft and Buses Regulations (SOR/2016-43; last amended 2022-09-27)
- Potable Water on Board Trains, Vessels, Aircraft and Buses Regulations (SOR/2016-43; last amended 2022-09-27)
- Financial Administration Act The purpose of this Act is to provide the legal framework for financial management within the Government of Canada. This Act provides the Minister of Health with the authority to prescribe the fees for the examination of an application for, or the renewal of a dealer's licence. Fees in Respect of Dealer's Licences Regulations (SOR/2011-79; last amended 2020-04-01) Licensed Dealers for Controlled Drugs and Narcotics (Veterinary Use) Fees Regulations (SOR/98-5; last amended 2022-06-03)

- Food and Drugs Act This Act applies to all food, drugs, cosmetics and medical devices sold in Canada, whether manufactured in Canada or imported. The Act and regulations contribute to ensuring the safety of and prevent deception in relation to food, drugs, cosmetics and medical devices by governing their sale and advertisement. - Hazardous Materials Information Review Act This Act establishes the mechanism to protect confidential business information from the disclosure requirements of the Hazardous Products Act , Canada Labour Code , and provincial and territorial occupational health and safety Acts. Hazardous Materials Information Review Regulations (SOR/88-456; last amended 2020-03-18)

- Hazardous Products Act This Act establishes the supplier label and safety data sheet requirements of the Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System, WHMIS. Hazardous Products Regulations (SOR/2015-17; last amended 2022-12-15)
- Hazardous Products Regulations (SOR/2015-17; last amended 2022-12-15)
- Patent Act This Act establishes the Patented Medicine Prices Review Board (PMPRB) with a mandate to regulate the prices of patented medicines sold in Canada to ensure that they are not excessive and to report to Parliament annually through the Minister of Health. Under the Act, Health Canada has also introduced regulations that establish Certificates of Supplementary Protection for medicinal ingredients in Canadian pharmaceuticals, biologics and veterinary drugs. - Pest Control Products Act This Act and the associated regulations apply to all pesticides manufactured, possessed, handled, stored, transported, imported, distributed or used in Canada. Pesticides include a variety of products such as insecticides, herbicides and fungicides for use in agriculture, forestry, industry and households. This Act and the regulations protect human health and the environment through a pre- and post-market evaluation program to prevent unacceptable risks. Health Canada's Pest Management Regulatory Agency is responsible for administering this legislation. - Pesticide Residue Compensation Act This Act sets up a compensation regime under which the Minister of Health may compensate a farmer for losses attributable to the use of a pesticide in accordance with the label directions. Assessor's Rules of Procedure (SOR/2003-293; last amended 2007-05-17) Pesticide Residue Compensation Regulations (C.R.C., c. 1254)

- Radiation Emitting Devices Act This Act and regulations govern the sale, lease, advertising, and importation of radiation emitting devices. The Act helps protect human health from radiation risks posed by devices that emit electromagnetic and acoustic radiation. Radiation Emitting Devices Regulations (C.R.C., c. 1370; last amended 2022-09-27)
- Radiation Emitting Devices Regulations (C.R.C., c. 1370; last amended 2022-09-27)
- Tobacco and Vaping Products Act This Act aims to protect the health of Canadians in light of conclusive evidence implicating tobacco use in the incidence of numerous debilitating and fatal diseases; to protect young persons and others from inducements to use tobacco products and the consequent dependence on them; to protect young persons and non-users of tobacco from inducements to use vaping products; to protect the health of young persons by restricting access to tobacco products and vaping products; to prevent the public from being misled with respect to the health hazards from using tobacco products or vaping products; and to enhance public awareness of those health hazards.
